ticket.subject: Fwd: Important update: mandatory receiver details timing
ticket.raw_subject: Fwd: Important update: mandatory receiver details timing
ticket.description:
Hi Team, 

Please share this with L3 once. If they have received the message, we can move the ticket to pending.

Best Regards,

Abhilash S | Digital Marketing & Partnerships
Connect with us on LinkedIn <https://www.linkedin.com/company/Pluginhive>

> Begin forwarded message:
> 
> From: PartnerManagement <PartnerManagement@auspost.com.au>
> Subject: Important update: mandatory receiver details timing
> Date: 15 May 2026 at 8:01:28 AM GMT+5:30
> To: PartnerManagement <PartnerManagement@auspost.com.au>
> 
> More time to prepare for changes
>  
>  <https://auspost.com.au/>	
>  
>  
>  
> Recipient details will be mandatory from Tuesday 4 August
> 
>  
>  
>  
>  
>  
> Dear Valued Partner,
>  
> We wrote to you in April to advise that the customer requirement to capture accurate recipient emails or Australian mobile numbers, and the ability to opt out from sending non-essential tracking notifications would become effective on 31 May 2026.
>  
> Today, we’re reaching out with an update on that timing and to advise that this requirement will now be effective from Tuesday 4 August. 
>  
> We made this decision after listening to our mutual customers, who told us they need more time to make the changes required to capture these mandatory recipient details. We have also advised our merchant customers of the same today. Alongside these changes, we’re introducing simpler, easier-to-rememberParcel Locker and Post Office collection point addresses. 
>  
> Why we need recipient details
>  
> The provision of an accurate recipient email or Australian mobile number for domestic manifested shipments via eParcel and MyPost Business allows us to notify all recipients, when required, that their parcel can be collected from a Parcel Locker or Post Office. This mandatory requirement also applies to parcels addressed to a Parcel Locker.
>  
> Validation rules will only allow for Australian formatted mobile numbers to be accepted. The acceptable formats are listed below for ease of reference:  
>  
> Australian mobile number 
> Australian mobile in international format 
> Versions with brackets 
> 04XX XXX XXX 
> +61 4XX XXX XXX 
> (04XX) XXX XXX 
> 04XX-XXX-XXX 
> +61 4XX-XXX-XXX 
> (+61) 4XX XXX XXX 
> 04XXXXXXXX 
> +614XXXXXXXX 
> (+61) 4XX-XXX-XXX 
>  
>  
> (+61) 4XXXXXXXX 
>  
> What happens from Tuesday 4 August
>  
> From Tuesday 4 August, the inclusion of recipient contact details will become mandatory for most Australia Post parcel delivery services (including Parcel Post, Express Post, Metro, and On Demand) sent by eParcel Contract merchants and customers using MyPost Business. Senders will need to provide an accurate <> recipient Australian mobile number or email address.
>  
> If customers don’t provide an accurate recipient email or Australian mobile number, they may not be able to submit a manifest or create a consignment and in some instances, they will not be able to print a label or pay for postage. For eParcel customers, as per their existing Service Agreement, if they send an unmanifested parcel, they may become liable for unmanifested parcel charges.
>  
> To enable this, and ensure that the article is successfully manifested, you must capture and pass on accurate receiver email or mobile number from customers.
>  
> The capture of mobile number or email is not mandatory for StarTrack Premium, Fixed Price Premium, StarTrack Express, and eParcel Returns, however it is highly recommended.
>  
> We are also deferring until Tuesday 4 August, the availability of the functionality that will allow senders to ‘switch off’ non-essential tracking notifications from Australia Post (Opt Out function). Please do not use this flag in the production environment prior to the date noted above.
>  
> API Testbed
>  
> Making the receiver contact details mandatory requires a change to our Shipping & Tracking APIs. These changes are live in the API testbed. 
>  
> You can also find out how to implement the notifications ‘Opt Out’ function in the Developer Centre <https://developers.auspost.com.au/apis/shipping-and-tracking/reference/create-domestic-shipments>.
>  
> We’re here to help
>  
> For general questions about these changes, please contact our eCommerce Partnerships team. <mailto:partners@auspost.com.au?subject=Important%20update:%20mandatory%20receiver%20details%20timing>
>  
> For technical queries regarding changes to the Shipping & Tracking APIs, please contact the relevant team below:
>  
> MyPost Business: contact our MPB Partner Support Team <mailto:mpbpartnersupport@auspost.com.au?subject=Important%20update:%20mandatory%20receiver%20details%20timing>
> Contract Services Lodgement Support team (eParcel & StarTrack): complete the form available at auspost.com.au/lodgement-techsupport <https://auspost.com.au/lodgement-techsupport>
>  
>  
> Regards,
> 
> The eCommerce Partnerships team
>  
>  
>  
>  
>  
>  
>  
>  
> 
> Australia Post will never send you an unsolicited email asking for your password, credit card details or account information.
>  
>  
>  
> This email was sent by Australia Post, 480 Swan Street, RICHMOND VIC 3121 <https://www.google.com/maps/search/480+Swan+Street,+RICHMOND+VIC+3121?entry=gmail&source=g>. This is a service communication. If you have any concerns, or don't think you should have received this email, please call us on 13 POST (13 7678). For more information, visit Australia Post – Online security <https://click.email.auspost.com.au/?qs=d8067f7b5b2a0a3005457dba2aebd785715c177257f9d7510ccd45967f0b2e70952fec89a82c6a96ea7e57e53c414e8c9e5fa3c37fd44fed>.
> 
> If you do not wish to receive such information please contact our Partner team <mailto:Partners@auspost.com.au?subject=Unsubscribe%20from%20partner%20notifications>. Australia Post does not represent, warrant or guarantee that the integrity of this email communication has been maintained nor that the communication is free of errors, viruses or interference.
>  
> Terms and Conditions <https://auspost.com.au/terms-conditions>     Help & Support <https://auspost.com.au/help-and-support>     Privacy Statement <https://auspost.com.au/privacy>    
>  
>  
>  
> © 2026 Australia Post. All Rights Reserved.
>  
>  
>  
>  
>  
> 
> Australia Post acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of the land on which we operate, live and gather as employees, and recognise their continuing connection to land, water and community. We pay respect to Elders past, present and emerging.
>  
>  
> 
>  
> Australia Post is committed to providing our customers with excellent service. If we can assist you in any way please telephone 13 13 18 or visit our website.
> The information contained in this email communication may be proprietary, confidential or legally professionally privileged. It is intended exclusively for the individual or entity to which it is addressed. You should only read, disclose, re-transmit, copy, distribute, act in reliance on or commercialise the information if you are authorised to do so. Australia Post does not represent, warrant or guarantee that the integrity of this email communication has been maintained nor that the communication is free of errors, virus or interference. 
> If you are not the addressee or intended recipient please notify us by replying direct to the sender and then destroy any electronic or paper copy of this message. Any views expressed in this email communication are taken to be those of the individual sender, except where the sender specifically attributes those views to Australia Post and is authorised to do so.
> Please consider the environment before printing this email.
>

>

>

> Dear Valued Partner,
>
>
>
> We wrote to you in April to advise that the customer requirement to capture accurate recipient emails or Australian mobile numbers, and the ability to opt out from sending non-essential tracking notifications would become effective on 31 May 2026.
>
>
>
> Today, we’re reaching out with an update on that timing and to advise that this** requirement will now be effective from Tuesday 4 August**.
>
>
>
> We made this decision after listening to our mutual customers, who told us they need more time to make the changes required to capture these mandatory recipient details. We have also advised our merchant customers of the same today. Alongside these changes, we’re introducing simpler, easier-to-rememberParcel Locker and Post Office collection point addresses.
>
> ** **
>
> **Why we need recipient details**
>
> ** **
>
> The provision of an accurate recipient email or Australian mobile number for domestic manifested shipments via eParcel and MyPost Business allows us to notify all recipients, when required, that their parcel can be collected from a Parcel Locker or Post Office. This mandatory requirement also applies to parcels addressed to a Parcel Locker.
>
>
>
> Validation rules will only allow for Australian formatted mobile numbers to be accepted. The acceptable formats are listed below for ease of reference:
>
>
>
> **Australian mobile number**
> **Australian mobile in international format**
> **Versions with brackets**
>
> 04XX XXX XXX
> +61 4XX XXX XXX
> (04XX) XXX XXX
>
> 04XX-XXX-XXX
> +61 4XX-XXX-XXX
> (+61) 4XX XXX XXX

>
>

>
>
>

>
> ** **
>
> From Tuesday 4 August, the inclusion of **recipient contact details will become mandatory** for most Australia Post parcel delivery services (including Parcel Post, Express Post, Metro, and On Demand) sent by eParcel Contract merchants and customers using MyPost Business. Senders will need to provide an accurate recipient Australian mobile number or email address.
>
>
>
> If customers don’t provide an accurate recipient email or Australian mobile number, they may **not be able to submit a manifest or create a consignment **and in some instances, they will **not be able to print a label or pay for postage. **For eParcel customers, as per their existing Service Agreement, if they send an unmanifested parcel, they may become liable for unmanifested parcel charges.
>
>
>
> To enable this, and ensure that the article is successfully manifested, you must capture and pass on accurate receiver email or mobile number from customers.
>
>
>
> The capture of mobile number or email is not mandatory for StarTrack Premium, Fixed Price Premium, StarTrack Express, and eParcel Returns, however it is **highly recommended.**
>
>
>
> We are also deferring until Tuesday 4 August, the availability of the functionality that will allow senders to ‘switch off’ non-essential tracking notifications from Australia Post **(Opt Out function)**. **Please do not use this flag **in the production environment** **prior to the date noted above.
>
>
>
> **API Testbed**
>
>
>
> Making the receiver contact details mandatory requires a change to our Shipping & Tracking APIs. These changes are live in the API testbed.
>
>
>
> You can also find out how to implement the notifications ‘Opt Out’ function in the Developer Centre.
>
>
>
> **We’re here to help**

>
>
>
> For technical queries regarding changes to the Shipping & Tracking APIs, please contact the relevant team below:

> - Contract Services Lodgement Support team (eParcel & StarTrack): complete the form available at auspost.com.au/lodgement-techsupport

> Regards,

comments.html_body:
Hi Team, 

Please share this with L3 once. If they have received the message, we can move the ticket to pending.

Best Regards,

Abhilash S | Digital Marketing & Partnerships
Connect with us on LinkedIn

Begin forwarded message:

From: PartnerManagement <PartnerManagement@auspost.com.au>
Subject: Important update: mandatory receiver details timing
Date: 15 May 2026 at 8:01:28 AM GMT+5:30
To: PartnerManagement <PartnerManagement@auspost.com.au>

More time to prepare for changes
 
 
 

 

Recipient details will be mandatory from Tuesday 4 August

 

 
 
 
 
Dear Valued Partner,
 
We wrote to you in April to advise that the customer requirement to capture accurate recipient emails or Australian mobile numbers, and the ability to opt out from sending non-essential tracking notifications would become effective on 31 May 2026.
 
Today, we’re reaching out with an update on that timing and to advise that this requirement will now be effective from Tuesday 4 August. 
 
We made this decision after listening to our mutual customers, who told us they need more time to make the changes required to capture these mandatory recipient details. We have also advised our merchant customers of the same today. Alongside these changes, we’re introducing simpler, easier-to-rememberParcel Locker and Post Office collection point addresses. 
 
Why we need recipient details
 
The provision of an accurate recipient email or Australian mobile number for domestic manifested shipments via eParcel and MyPost Business allows us to notify all recipients, when required, that their parcel can be collected from a Parcel Locker or Post Office. This mandatory requirement also applies to parcels addressed to a Parcel Locker.
 
Validation rules will only allow for Australian formatted mobile numbers to be accepted. The acceptable formats are listed below for ease of reference:  
 
Australian mobile number 
Australian mobile in international format 
Versions with brackets 
04XX XXX XXX 
+61 4XX XXX XXX 
(04XX) XXX XXX 
04XX-XXX-XXX 
+61 4XX-XXX-XXX 
(+61) 4XX XXX XXX 
04XXXXXXXX 
+614XXXXXXXX 
(+61) 4XX-XXX-XXX 
 
 
(+61) 4XXXXXXXX 
 
What happens from Tuesday 4 August
 
From Tuesday 4 August, the inclusion of recipient contact details will become mandatory for most Australia Post parcel delivery services (including Parcel Post, Express Post, Metro, and On Demand) sent by eParcel Contract merchants and customers using MyPost Business. Senders will need to provide an accurate recipient Australian mobile number or email address.
 
If customers don’t provide an accurate recipient email or Australian mobile number, they may not be able to submit a manifest or create a consignment and in some instances, they will not be able to print a label or pay for postage. For eParcel customers, as per their existing Service Agreement, if they send an unmanifested parcel, they may become liable for unmanifested parcel charges.
 
To enable this, and ensure that the article is successfully manifested, you must capture and pass on accurate receiver email or mobile number from customers.
 
The capture of mobile number or email is not mandatory for StarTrack Premium, Fixed Price Premium, StarTrack Express, and eParcel Returns, however it is highly recommended.
 
We are also deferring until Tuesday 4 August, the availability of the functionality that will allow senders to ‘switch off’ non-essential tracking notifications from Australia Post (Opt Out function). Please do not use this flag in the production environment prior to the date noted above.
 
API Testbed
 
Making the receiver contact details mandatory requires a change to our Shipping & Tracking APIs. These changes are live in the API testbed. 
 
You can also find out how to implement the notifications ‘Opt Out’ function in the Developer Centre.
 
We’re here to help
 
For general questions about these changes, please contact our eCommerce Partnerships team.
 
For technical queries regarding changes to the Shipping & Tracking APIs, please contact the relevant team below:
 
  • MyPost Business: contact our MPB Partner Support Team
  • Contract Services Lodgement Support team (eParcel & StarTrack): complete the form available at auspost.com.au/lodgement-techsupport
 
 
Regards,

The eCommerce Partnerships team

 

 

 
 
 
 
 

 
Australia Post will never send you an unsolicited email asking for your password, credit card details or account information.

 

 
 
This email was sent by Australia Post, 480 Swan Street, RICHMOND VIC 3121. This is a service communication. If you have any concerns, or don't think you should have received this email, please call us on 13 POST (13 7678). For more information, visit Australia Post – Online security.

If you do not wish to receive such information please contact our Partner team. Australia Post does not represent, warrant or guarantee that the integrity of this email communication has been maintained nor that the communication is free of errors, viruses or interference.
 
Terms and Conditions     Help & Support     Privacy Statement    
 

 

 
© 2026 Australia Post. All Rights Reserved.
 
 
 

 
 
Australia Post acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of the land on which we operate, live and gather as employees, and recognise their continuing connection to land, water and community. We pay respect to Elders past, present and emerging.

 

 
 

Australia Post is committed to providing our customers with excellent service. If we can assist you in any way please telephone 13 13 18 or visit our website.
The information contained in this email communication may be proprietary, confidential or legally professionally privileged. It is intended exclusively for the individual or entity to which it is addressed. You should only read, disclose, re-transmit, copy, distribute, act in reliance on or commercialise the information if you are authorised to do so. Australia Post does not represent, warrant or guarantee that the integrity of this email communication has been maintained nor that the communication is free of errors, virus or interference. 
If you are not the addressee or intended recipient please notify us by replying direct to the sender and then destroy any electronic or paper copy of this message. Any views expressed in this email communication are taken to be those of the individual sender, except where the sender specifically attributes those views to Australia Post and is authorised to do so.
Please consider the environment before printing this email.

comments.body:
Recipient phone number will become mandatory from August 2026 for both eParcel and MPB.

In the Australia Post app, we currently pass the recipient phone number only if it is provided during checkout. We need to make this field mandatory and display a warning/error when the phone number is missing.

In MCSL, if the phone number is not entered at checkout, we currently pass the value as `null`. This also requires sanitization, and the app should display a warning/error whenever the recipient phone number is not available.

Moving to dev_needed.
[Veena]
comments.html_body:
Recipient phone number will become mandatory from August 2026 for both eParcel and MPB.
 
In the Australia Post app, we currently pass the recipient phone number only if it is provided during checkout. We need to make this field mandatory and display a warning/error when the phone number is missing.
 
In MCSL, if the phone number is not entered at checkout, we currently pass the value as null. This also requires sanitization, and the app should display a warning/error whenever the recipient phone number is not available.
 
Moving to dev_needed.
[Veena]
